claim: Enumerated 60 Z-primitive irreducible quartics b(n) with a4 in {1,2,3,5,7}, a3,a2,a1,a0 in {-3..3}, lex order; bin counts: {'mixed_S_4': 57, 'mixed_D_4': 2, '+_real_D_4': 1}; Galois histogram: {'S_4': 57, 'D_4': 3}
Calibration anchors: x^4-2 -> D_4 (Eisenstein), x^4+1 -> V_4 (Phi_8 biquadratic), x^4-x-1 -> S_4; all anchors match expected Galois group
WKB-quartic harvest (60 cataloged families): 60/60 within 0.10 of A=2d=8; mean=7.9544, sigma=0.0037, min=7.9488, max=7.9605; verdict counts: {'b4_consistent_at_8': 60}
Newton-polygon at d=4: characteristic root xi_0(b) = 4 / alpha_4^(1/4) for all 8 representative quartics (catalogue + 3 anchors + alpha_4=7 sample); c(4) = 4, spread = 0.0; claim Q1-B SUPPORTED

General xi_0 universality (deduced from operator L = 1 - z B(theta+1) - z^2): xi_0(b) = d / beta_d^{1/d} for any degree-d PCF with a_n = 1; recovers d=2 prop:xi0 (xi_0=2/sqrt(beta_2)) and predicts d=3 xi_0=3/beta_3^{1/3}
